concept.headline: Crystal concurrency uses lightweight fibers and typed channels.
concept.explanation: spawn starts concurrent fibers. Channel(T) provides safe message passing, avoiding shared mutable-state complexity.
concept.syntax:
ch = Channel(String).new
spawn do
  ch.send("done")
end
puts ch.receive

demo.intro: Observe one fiber sending a message and main fiber receiving it.
demo.code:
ch = Channel(String).new
spawn do
  ch.send("done")
end
puts ch.receive

explanation.misconception: Channel enforces message type at compile time
spawn is lightweight compared to threads
send may block depending on channel state

explanation.why: main fiber waits for incoming value

prediction.question: What happens if main calls ch.receive before any fiber sends?
prediction.explanation: receive blocks until a message is available.

explanation.afterDemo: Fibers plus channels are a practical way to build concurrent pipelines with less shared-state risk.
keyTakeaways: spawn runs concurrent tasks as fibers.
Channels coordinate and move typed data safely.
receive is blocking synchronization by default.

practice.prompt: Create Channel(Int32). Spawn a fiber sending 21, receive it in main, print value * 2.
practice.starterCode:
# create channel
# spawn sender
# receive and print doubled
practice.solutionCode:
ch = Channel(Int32).new
spawn do
  ch.send(21)
end
value = ch.receive
puts value * 2
practice.hint: Receive into value, then multiply by 2.

feedback.successMessage: Excellent concurrency flow: spawn, send, receive, process.
